name: OSRM (Open Source Routing Machine)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD 2-Clause); self-hosted; no API costs. Demo server for testing only.

auth.notes: Self-hosted; no auth by default. Add reverse proxy auth for production.

performance.notes: Sub-10ms routing queries after preprocessing; preprocessing can take hours for planet-scale OSM data

agent_readiness.notes: High-performance routing engine using OpenStreetMap data. HTTP API: route, nearest, table (distance matrix), match (map matching), trip (TSP), tile (vector tiles). Contraction Hierarchies (CH) or Multi-Level Dijkstra (MLD) algorithm. Python/JS client libraries available. Widely used in logistics, navigation, and isochrone calculation.

gotchas: Preprocessing OSM data requires significant time and memory (planet = 64GB+ RAM)
No real-time traffic data — static routing based on OSM speed profiles
Routing profiles (car/bike/foot) require custom Lua scripts for modifications
Map updates require full re-preprocessing of OSM data
best_when: Self-hosted routing with privacy requirements (no third-party API calls)
High-volume routing (millions of requests) where commercial APIs are cost-prohibitive
Distance matrix calculations for logistics optimization (table service)
Map matching GPS traces to road network
avoid_when: Real-time traffic-aware routing (use HERE, Google Maps, or Valhalla with traffic)
Small-scale use cases (hosted OSRM demo or GraphHopper/OSRM cloud services simpler)
summary: OSRM is a high-performance self-hosted routing engine using OpenStreetMap data. Provides route, table (distance matrix), match (GPS trace), and trip (TSP) services via HTTP API. Sub-10ms queries after preprocessing. Best for high-volume routing workloads requiring data privacy or cost control. Requires significant infrastructure for large-scale OSM data preprocessing.
